title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While inspecting the property known as Mountain Slope Villa, Gwendraith experiences a sudden, inexplicable sense of fear. She asks the owner, Mrs. Hargreaves, if the house is haunted or if anyone has died there. Mrs. Hargreaves responds icily, noting that while people have died in the century-old house, the previous owner, Miss Elvosi, was in good health. Mrs. Hargreaves explains she is in a hurry to move to Kent to be with her own kind. After touring the neglected garden and terrace, Gwendraith decides to make an offer on the villa. Following a stroll through Dartmouth, she learns the real estate agent has accepted her offer, and she sends a telegram to her husband, Jervis, to announce the purchase.

A month later, Gwendraith moves into the villa, furnishing it with high-quality items from her aunt's estate. While renovations are ongoing, she hires Mrs. Cochran to manage the kitchen. Mrs. Cochran is a well-mannered woman who disapproves of Gwendraith's friendliness toward lower social classes. Gwendraith settles into a small, round bedroom that Mrs. Cochran suggests may eventually serve as a nursery. While exploring the room, Gwendraith discovers a locked closet that has been painted shut for years. Later, Gwendraith goes to the garden to check on Foster, the part-time gardener. She insists he carve a path through the rockery, which requires removing various bushes. During their interaction, Foster tells Gwendraith that it looks as if she is "back in the past," leaving her questioning his meaning.
